title: Jenkins Segment 02
summary: A substantial Jenkins core development segment spanning 84 PRs across 16 dependency layers. This segment covers major UI modernization (SCSS/JS overhaul with component redesign), core Java model improvements (Jenkins, Computer, Run, Queue, Job, Descriptor, AdministrativeMonitor), security enhancements (CSRF, API tokens, agent protocols), WebSocket support, CLI refinements, and build system updates. The UI work introduces modern styling for forms, tables, tooltips, dropdowns, dialogs, spinners, and the command palette.
instructionPreview: `/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.
instruction:
You are an autonomous engineer. The repository at /workspace is at an earlier state of a real-world project.

`/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.

moduleDag.description: A substantial Jenkins core development segment spanning 84 PRs across 16 dependency layers. This segment covers major UI modernization (SCSS/JS overhaul with component redesign), core Java model improvements (Jenkins, Computer, Run, Queue, Job, Descriptor, AdministrativeMonitor), security enhancements (CSRF, API tokens, agent protocols), WebSocket support, CLI refinements, and build system updates. The UI work introduces modern styling for forms, tables, tooltips, dropdowns, dialogs, spinners, and the command palette.

nodes.label: Core Java (Server Logic)
nodes.description: The Jenkins server-side Java codebase covering models (Jenkins.java with 26 PRs of edge overlap, Computer, Run, Queue, Job, Descriptor, AbstractItem, User), security (CSRF, HudsonPrivateSecurityRealm, agent protocols), utilities (AtomicFileWriter, FormValidation, HttpResponses), slaves/agents, CLI handlers, and logging (LogRecorder). This is the most heavily modified area with 360 unique files touched.

nodes.label: Core Resources (Jelly/Properties)
nodes.description: Server-side view templates (Jelly/Groovy), message properties, help files, and configuration forms. Covers views for Run, Jenkins, Computer, UpdateCenter, user properties, security configuration, and project views. 1119 resource files reflecting UI restructuring and i18n updates.

nodes.label: WAR SCSS (Core Styles)
nodes.description: Core stylesheet modernization: abstracts/_theme.scss, form/_checkbox.scss, form/_layout.scss, components/_table.scss, components/_tooltips.scss, components/_spinner.scss, components/_dropdowns.scss, components/_dialogs.scss, and page-specific styles for jobs, builds, and configuration screens.

nodes.label: Frontend SCSS (Design System)
nodes.description: Design system stylesheets: component styles (buttons, cards, badges, breadcrumbs, side-panel, search, tabs), form styles (input, select, radio), page layouts, base resets, and abstract variables. Provides the styling foundation.

nodes.label: WAR JavaScript
nodes.description: Client-side JavaScript in the WAR package: page-specific scripts for project/builds-card, configuration, log recording, and shared utilities. Handles dynamic UI behavior for the Jenkins web interface.

nodes.label: Frontend JavaScript (Components)
nodes.description: Modular JS components: command-palette, dropdowns (combo-box, autocomplete, jumplists), template system (handlebars helpers), page scripts, utility modules (keyboard, accessibility), and API layer. Powers interactive UI features.

nodes.label: Build Configuration
nodes.description: Maven POMs (root pom.xml, bom/pom.xml, war/pom.xml, cli/pom.xml), webpack configs, eslint config, ath.sh (acceptance test harness), packaging-ref.txt, and dependency management. Controls the build pipeline and dependency versions.

nodes.label: WAR Web Application Resources
nodes.description: Static web resources: hudson-behavior.js (10 edge references), CSS, images, WEB-INF configuration, and legacy webapp assets. Provides the base web application scaffold that newer JS/SCSS builds extend.

nodes.label: WAR Packaging & Resources
nodes.description: WAR module non-JS/SCSS content: Java sources (executable/Main.java), i18n message resources, PostCSS config, and WAR-specific build configuration.

nodes.label: WebSocket Support
nodes.description: WebSocket SPI and implementations for Jetty 10, Jetty 12 EE8, and Jetty 12 EE9. Provides real-time communication channels for agent connectivity and streaming.

nodes.label: CLI Module
nodes.description: Jenkins CLI client: command implementations, connection handling, and client-side logic for remote Jenkins management. Updated for protocol and security changes.

edges.label: Jelly views render Java model objects and bind to form fields
WAR styles import and extend the design system abstracts
WAR scripts consume shared frontend components
webapp provides base layout that Jelly views render into
JS components attach to DOM elements defined in webapp
WebSocket SPI implementations integrate with Jenkins core
CLI commands invoke core Java APIs remotely
Maven builds produce WAR packaging artifacts
POM manages core dependencies and build lifecycle
webapp scripts (hudson-behavior.js) interact with server APIs
JS components reference CSS classes from design system
